metadata.title: Sogverse – School of Gaming
metadata.description: Where screen time becomes quality time through Minecraft clubs led by professional game educators.
metadata.shortDescription: School of Gaming – Where screen time becomes quality time

pages.yty: Yty – the Force of the Sogverse

common.noPermission: You don't have permission to view this page.

hero.title: About <primary>School of Gaming</primary>
hero.subtitle: In our Minecraft clubs, screen time becomes quality time. We bring together children, professional game educators, and the game kids already love — creating playful learning experiences where they make new friends, develop real skills, and have fun.

quote.text: “What is true now, was once just your imagination.”
quote.attribution: — The Principal of the School of Gaming

mission.heading: Our Mission
mission.text: All screen content is not made equal. Gaming is a great hobby when it is treated like all the other hobbies. We use children's love for games to their advantage — with good friends and a professional game educator who is there to guide and help.

playIsEssential.title: Play Is Essential for Children
playIsEssential.description: Play is a child's work. It is crucial for their wellbeing and development. Our clubs are designed to promote free play that feeds imagination, curiosity, and creativity — powerful tools to face any future.

friendsCarry.title: Friends Carry Over Obstacles
friendsCarry.description: No one should be left without a friend. Moments of play and friendship carry kids over any obstacle in life. In our clubs, children build genuine connections through shared adventures.

keepChildrenSafe.title: Keep Children Safe Online
keepChildrenSafe.description: We nurture inclusive, kind, and safe online communities. We grow caring and polite digital citizens who look out for their fellow gamers. Zero tolerance for bullying and toxicity.

familyInTheLoop.title: Family in the Loop
familyInTheLoop.description: Parents are partners. You stay in control of your child's gaming experience while they enjoy the freedom to explore, create, and make friends in a safe environment.

howClubsWork.heading: How Our Clubs Work
howClubsWork.paragraph1: Each week, our gamers log in and are greeted by their own game educator — a Gedu. The Gedu guides them through the day's topic, giving children space and freedom to make the adventures their own.
howClubsWork.paragraph2: Our Gedus are gamers themselves. They understand firsthand that as children and teenagers, identity and friendships are built both online and offline. That understanding shapes every session they lead.
howClubsWork.paragraph3: All our clubs are built around stories that give space for gamers to explore, create, and imagine. Between weekly sessions, gamers get fun challenges to do online and offline. During holidays we organise camps, and every week there are community events, tournaments, and competitions.
howClubsWork.paragraph4: Every gamer takes an oath to behave kindly and righteously — whether in-game, on voice chat, or out in the real world. The same values apply everywhere.

forParents.heading: For Parents
forParents.paragraph1: Parental game education is just as important a part of what we do as the clubs themselves. We want to help parents keep up with the ever-changing gaming world so they can support their children with confidence.
forParents.paragraph2: You stay in control — managing accounts, enrolments, and spending — while your child enjoys a safe environment guided by professionals who care.

cta.heading: Join Our Community
cta.subheading: Give your child a fun, safe place to play, learn, and make friends — guided by a professional game educator.

dashboard.title: Admin Dashboard
dashboard.description: Welcome to the Sogverse admin panel. Manage users, products, and system settings.

products.title: Products
products.noProducts: No products yet. Create your first product.
products.hideConfirm: Are you sure you want to hide "{name}"? It will no longer appear in the catalogue.
products.showConfirm: Are you sure you want to show "{name}"? It will appear in the catalogue for parents.
products.addGroupBeforeVisible: Add at least one gedu group before making this product visible.
products.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
products.createNewProduct: Create a new product for the catalogue
products.fillDetails: Fill in the product details below.

consumerClub.label: Consumer club
consumerClub.tagline: Weekly, ongoing, parents pay per session.
consumerClub.blurb: Parents enroll their child and pay per session. Clubs run week after week with no end date unless you set one.

traits.3: Parents pay per session, with optional subscription rate
traits.4: Fixed seat count, with a waitlist

municipalityClub.label: Municipality club
municipalityClub.blurb: A municipality or school pays up front for a block of seats. Parents register their child for free; the registration window opens at a fixed moment like a concert ticket drop.

traits.2: Runs between a start and end date
traits.3: School of Gaming invoices the municipality
traits.4: Fixed seat count, with a waitlist

camp.label: Camp
camp.tagline: Multiple days for a set period. Parents pay upfront.
camp.blurb: A camp runs across several days of the week for a set period — any time of year. Parents pay the full price once, at signup. Refundable up to a cutoff before the camp starts.

traits.1: Several days per week (each with its own time)
traits.2: Runs between a start and end date

event.label: Event
event.tagline: A single date. Free, or a one-time charge.
event.blurb: A one-time event on a specific date. Good for demo days, community walks, webinars, tournaments. Free events can have no seat limit; paid events always have a seat count.

traits.3: Free events can have no seat limit

sections.identityDescription: What to call this and what it's about.

whereDescription.consumerClub: Online or in person. Online clubs are offered to anyone — no location needed. In-person clubs run at a specific site.
whereDescription.municipalityClub: Paid for by a specific municipality. Online clubs pick the municipality directly; in-person clubs pick a site within it.
whereDescription.camp: Online or in person. Online camps are offered to anyone — no location needed. In-person camps run at a specific site.
whereDescription.event: Online or in person. Online events are offered to anyone — no location needed. In-person events run at a specific site.

whenDescription.consumerClub: One day per week, one time. Leave the end date blank for an ongoing club.
whenDescription.municipalityClub: One day per week, one time. Runs between the start and end dates.
whenDescription.camp: Multiple days per week, each with its own start time and duration.
whenDescription.event: A single date and start time.

sections.groupsDescription: Admins use groups to organize participants inside a product. Parents don't see groups — they see the product.

billingDescription.consumerClub: Parents pay per session their child attends. Billing happens automatically each week.
billingDescription.municipalityClub: School of Gaming invoices the municipality directly. Parents register their child for free, up to the seat limit.
billingDescription.camp: Parents pay the total price once, at signup. Refundable up to a cutoff.
billingDescription.event: Events can be free (with or without a seat limit) or paid upfront.

sections.registrationDescription: Pick when parents can start signing up.
sections.visibilityDescription: Control whether parents can see this while you're still setting it up.

startModes.label: How does this start?
startModes.date_and_threshold: On a specific date, only if enough sign up
startModes.threshold: When enough gamers have signed up
startModes.dateDescription: Runs on the selected date, no matter how many parents sign up.
startModes.date_and_thresholdDescription: Scheduled for the selected date, but only runs if enough parents sign up in time. Otherwise you cancel it and parents are refunded automatically.
startModes.thresholdDescription: No fixed start date. You pick the first session date once enough parents have signed up — parents see a live counter and know you'll contact them when it's ready.

registrationModes.label: When does registration open?
registrationModes.immediatelyDescription: Parents can sign up the moment this product becomes visible.
registrationModes.scheduledDescription: Parents see a countdown until then. Useful for ticket-drop style launches.

hints.topicHint: The primary subject — one per product. What the sessions are actually about.
hints.tagsHint: Optional. Helps parents filter products when they're browsing.
hints.padletHint: Optional. Shared with families after they sign up.
hints.deliveredInHint: The primary language used during sessions.
hints.onlineNoLocation: Online and available to anyone — no location needed. Any parent browsing will see this, no matter where they live.
hints.voiceRoomAuto: A voice room will be set up for this product automatically.
hints.municipalityHint: Municipality clubs are always tied to a specific municipality — the one paying for it. Parents from that municipality (or anywhere smaller inside it) will see it when they browse.
hints.eventSingleDay: Events happen on a single date.
hints.endDateOpt: Leave blank for an ongoing club with no end date.
hints.thresholdOnly: No fixed start date yet. Parents see a "X of Y needed" counter while it fills up. You press Start once you have enough sign-ups and pick the first session date then.
hints.thresholdWithDate: If fewer than this many parents sign up by the start date, you'll need to cancel and refund from the product's page.
hints.timezoneFixedHelsinki: Times are in Europe/Helsinki. Parents see them converted to their local timezone automatically.
hints.holidayHint: No session will run on any date from the selected calendars. Edit a calendar once and every product using it updates automatically.
hints.groupsHint: Add groups now if you already know how you want to organize this — or leave this empty and add them later from the product page, once you see how many gamers sign up.
hints.groupsNotWired: Groups UI is in place but not yet wired to the backend — your group choices won't be saved on submit. Coming soon.
hints.billingHint: Free events can have no seat limit. Paid events always have a seat count.
hints.freeDetail: No charge. Anyone can join with one click.
hints.paidByMunicipality: No customer billing inside this system. School of Gaming invoices the municipality directly.
hints.pricingPerSession: Charged for each session the child attends. The monthly rate is what subscribers see — derived weekly/monthly from this number.
hints.pricingUpfront: Charged once at signup. Same total no matter the duration.
hints.seatHint: When full, new signups go to the waitlist.
hints.seatCanUncap: Free events can have no seat limit (e.g. a webinar, a public walk).
hints.visibleHint: Uncheck to keep the product hidden while you're still setting it up. Turn it on when you're ready for parents to see it.

minecraftApi.title: Minecraft Server API
minecraftApi.description: Use this endpoint to check whether a connecting player is allowed to join the Minecraft world. The server sends the player's Minecraft UUID and receives an allow/deny decision with session timing.

authentication.heading: Authentication
authentication.description: All requests require a static API key passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
authentication.contactNote: Contact the Sogverse team if you need a key or need it rotated.

endpoint.heading: Endpoint
endpoint.uuidDescription: The player's Minecraft UUID. Both dashed (<code1>069a79f4-44e9-4726-a5be-fca90e38aaf5</code1>) and undashed (<code2>069a79f444e94726a5befca90e38aaf5</code2>) formats are accepted.

responses.heading: Responses
responses.deniedDescription: The player exists in Sogverse but has no active session right now. No endTime is included.

fields.allowedTrue: Player has an active session right now.
fields.endTime: When the session window closes (includes a 5-minute buffer after the scheduled end). Use this to schedule a kick or re-check.
fields.reason: Human-readable description: the product name and educator name.

responses.error401: Missing, malformed, or invalid API key.
responses.error404: No Sogverse account linked to this Minecraft UUID.

integrationNotes.heading: Integration Notes
integrationNotes.note1: Call this endpoint on every player join. Both gedus and gamers are session-gated — they can only join during their scheduled session window.
integrationNotes.note2: The session window opens 5 minutes before the scheduled start and closes 5 minutes after the scheduled end.
integrationNotes.note3: When <code1>allowed: true</code1>, use <code2>endTime</code2> to schedule a re-check or kick. If back-to-back sessions exist (e.g. one ends 14:00, next starts 14:05), the player gets kicked at the first session's end and rejoins for the next.
integrationNotes.note4: A 404 means the player hasn't linked their Minecraft account in Sogverse yet. You can show them a message to link their account in their profile settings.
integrationNotes.note5: All error responses follow the shape '{ "error": "message" }'.

hero.title:
Where

Screen Time

Becomes

Quality Time
hero.subtitle: Our skilfully designed Minecraft clubs promote healthy gaming as a hobby. World-class game educators make every session a playful learning experience where children make new friends, develop their unique talents, and have fun doing what they love.

features.heading: Make Gaming a Great Hobby
features.subheading: Gaming is a hobby just like any other — better with good friends and with a professional instructor who is there to guide and help.

minecraftClubs.title: Minecraft Clubs with Gedus
minecraftClubs.description: Our game educators — Gedus — are gamers themselves. They understand that as children, identity and friendships are built both online and offline. Every session is guided by someone who truly gets it.

screenTime.title: Screen Time Becomes Quality Time
screenTime.description: Skilfully designed clubs turn gaming into a playful learning experience where children develop real skills and have fun doing what they love.

newFriends.title: New Friends, Real Connections
newFriends.description: Children build genuine, lasting friendships through shared adventures. No one is left without a friend. The gamer's oath means treating each other with kindness — online and offline.

parents.title: Parents Are Part of It
parents.description: Parental game education is just as important as the clubs themselves. We keep you informed, involved, and equipped to support your child's gaming journey.

howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.subheading: Join a Minecraft club in three simple steps.

step1.description: Sign up as a parent and create gamer accounts for your children.

step2.description: Browse our clubs and enrol your gamers in the ones that fit their schedule and interests.

step3.title: Join the Adventure
step3.description: Your gamers log in each week, meet their Gedu, and dive into Minecraft adventures with new friends.

cta.heading: Ready to Turn Gaming into Learning?
cta.subheading: Give your child a fun, safe place to play, learn, and make friends — guided by a professional game educator.

hero.title: What is <primary>Sorg</primary>?
hero.subtitle: Sorg is the currency that powers your child's club sessions in the Sogverse. Parents purchase Sorgs and they are used to pay for weekly sessions — simple, transparent, and flexible.

overview.heading: How It Works
overview.text: Each club session costs a set number of Sorgs. When you enrol your child in a club, Sorgs are deducted automatically each week for their session. Buy a token pack or subscribe monthly — whichever suits your family best.

benefits.heading: Why Sorgs?
benefits.subheading: A simple, fair way to pay for your child's clubs

payPerSession.title: Pay Per Session
payPerSession.description: Sorgs are deducted once a week for each enrolled club. You only pay for the sessions your child attends — no long-term contracts.

fullTransparency.title: Full Transparency
fullTransparency.description: See exactly where every Sorg goes. Your parent dashboard shows your balance, transaction history, and upcoming charges.

flexibleTopUps.title: Flexible Top-Ups
flexibleTopUps.description: Buy a one-time token pack when you need it, or subscribe for a monthly allowance at a better rate. Change or cancel anytime.

safeByDesign.title: Safe by Design
safeByDesign.description: Sorgs exist only within the Sogverse. Only parents can see balances and make purchases — children are never exposed to the currency. No surprises.

isRealMoney.question: Is Sorg real money?
isRealMoney.answer: Sorgs are purchased with real money but exist only within the Sogverse. They cannot be transferred outside the platform, but if you ever want a refund on unused Sorgs, just contact our support team.

howSpent.question: How are Sorgs spent?
howSpent.answer: Each club session costs a set number of Sorgs, shown on the club page. Sorgs are automatically deducted once a week when your child's session is scheduled.

parentControl.question: Can parents control spending?
parentControl.answer: Yes. Sorgs are completely invisible to children. Only parents can see balances, purchase tokens, and view spending history in the parent dashboard.

doExpire.question: Do Sorgs expire?
doExpire.answer: No. Once purchased, Sorgs stay in your account until they are used for club sessions. There are no expiration dates or hidden fees.

refund.question: Can I get a refund?
refund.answer: If you unenrol a gamer at least 24 hours before the start of a session, the Sorgs for that session are automatically refunded to your balance. For anything else, contact our support team — we're happy to help.

cta.heading: Ready to Get Started?
cta.subheading: Create an account, grab some Sorgs, and enrol your child in their first club.

hero.title: What is <primary>Yty</primary>?
hero.subtitle: Yty is a magical force that maintains the delicate balance of the worlds of the Sogverse. By doing good things — learning new skills, making friends, behaving well, and participating in the community — gamers earn Yty for themselves and for everyone.

overview.heading: How Yty Works
overview.paragraph1: Every gamer starts at zero. From every session attended, every challenge completed, and every act of kindness shown, gamers earn Yty points. These points build up across four elements, each reflecting a different part of growing up well.
overview.paragraph2: As gamers collect Yty for themselves, they also increase the total Yty of the Sogverse — shaping the story and unlocking new possibilities for the whole community.

elements.heading: The Four Elements of Yty
elements.subheading: Each element reflects a gamer's relationship with a different part of their world

harmony.name: Harmony
harmony.detail: Who am I? Harmony is about accepting yourself as you are — strengths and weaknesses alike. It means learning to take care of your wellbeing, expressing your emotions, making good decisions, and understanding why rules and trust matter.

glow.name: Glow
glow.detail: How can I help others? Glow is about empathy, kindness, and building meaningful relationships. It means respecting differences, giving space to others, communicating well, and being the kind of person who lifts people up.

valor.name: Valor
valor.detail: How can I make a difference? Valor is about finding your place in the world and having the courage to contribute. It means teamwork, expressing your ideas, using your imagination, and working towards a better future — locally and globally.

wit.name: Wit
wit.detail: How do I navigate the digital world? Wit is about a healthy, critical relationship with games, media, and technology. It means understanding online behaviour, thinking critically about information, and balancing screen time with the rest of life.

earning.heading: How Gamers Earn Yty
earning.paragraph1: Yty points are earned through participation. Attending weekly club sessions, completing quests and challenges, showing kindness to fellow gamers, joining community events and camps — all of these contribute to a gamer's Yty.
earning.paragraph2: As points accumulate under specific achievement badges, gamers level up those badges — from bronze to silver, gold, platinum, and diamond. Every point also feeds into their overall Yty level and the element it belongs to.
earning.paragraph3: And it's not just gamers. Gedus, parents, and the entire community can take part in building the Yty of the Sogverse through events and shared activities.

cta.heading: Start Your Journey
cta.subheading: Join the Sogverse and start earning Yty. Every session, every friendship, every act of kindness makes a difference.

passwordReset.subject: Reset your Sogverse password
passwordReset.heading: Reset Your Password
passwordReset.body: We received a request to reset your Sogverse password. Click the button below to choose a new password.
passwordReset.expiry: This link will expire in 72 hours.
passwordReset.ignore: If you didn’t request a password reset, you can safely ignore this email. Your password will not be changed.

geduInvite.subject: You’re invited to the Sogverse
geduInvite.heading: Welcome to the Sogverse
geduInvite.body: You’ve been invited to join the Sogverse as a Gedu. Click the button below to set up your account.
geduInvite.expiry: This link will expire in 72 hours.

enrollmentParent.subject: {gamerName} is now enrolled in {productName}
enrollmentParent.heading: Enrolment Confirmed
enrollmentParent.body: Hi {parentName}, {gamerName} is now enrolled in {productName} with Gedu {geduName}.

enrollmentGedu.subject: {gamerName} has joined your group – {productName}
enrollmentGedu.heading: New Gamer in Your Group
enrollmentGedu.body: Hi {geduName}, {gamerName} has joined your group for {productName}.

unenrollmentGedu.subject: {gamerName} has left your group – {productName}
unenrollmentGedu.heading: Gamer Left Your Group
unenrollmentGedu.body: Hi {geduName}, {gamerName} has left your group for {productName}.

groupAdded.subject: You’ve been assigned to a new group – {productName}
groupAdded.heading: You’ve been assigned to a new group
groupAdded.body: Hi {geduName}, you’ve been assigned to a new group for {productName}.
groupAdded.dashboardNote: You’ll see this group in your dashboard shortly.

groupDeleted.subject: Your group has been removed – {productName}
groupDeleted.heading: Your group has been removed
groupDeleted.body: Hi {geduName}, your group for {productName} has been removed by an admin.

groupReassignedOldGedu.subject: Your group has been reassigned – {productName}
groupReassignedOldGedu.heading: Your group has been reassigned
groupReassignedOldGedu.body: Hi {oldGeduName}, your group for {productName} has been reassigned to {newGeduName}.

groupReassignedNewGedu.subject: You’ve been assigned to a group – {productName}
groupReassignedNewGedu.heading: You’ve been assigned to a group
groupReassignedNewGedu.body: Hi {newGeduName}, you’ve been assigned to a group for {productName} (previously led by {oldGeduName}).

groupReassignedParent.subject: Gedu change for {gamerName} – {productName}
groupReassignedParent.heading: Your gamer’s Gedu has changed
groupReassignedParent.body: Hi {parentName}, the Gedu for {gamerName}’s group in {productName} has changed from {oldGeduName} to {newGeduName}.

gamerMovedParent.subject: {gamerName} has been moved to a new group – {productName}
gamerMovedParent.heading: Your child has been moved to a new group
gamerMovedParent.body: Hi {parentName}, {gamerName} has been moved from {oldGeduName}’s group to {newGeduName}’s group in {productName}.

gamerMovedOldGedu.subject: {gamerName} has been moved from your group – {productName}
gamerMovedOldGedu.heading: A gamer has been moved from your group
gamerMovedOldGedu.body: Hi {geduName}, {gamerName} has been moved from your group to {newGeduName}’s group in {productName}.

gamerMovedNewGedu.subject: {gamerName} has been moved to your group – {productName}
gamerMovedNewGedu.heading: A gamer has been moved to your group
gamerMovedNewGedu.body: Hi {geduName}, {gamerName} has been moved to your group from {oldGeduName}’s group in {productName}.

feedback.subject: Feedback from {displayName} ({role})
feedback.heading: New Feedback Received
feedback.gamerNote: This feedback is from a gamer account. Replies will go to their parent ({parentEmail}).

mouseflow.title: Help us improve Sogverse
mouseflow.body: Sogverse is in Beta. To learn how people actually use the site — so we can make it better for everyone — we'd like to record your visit (mouse movements, clicks, and which pages you visit). Only Sogverse admins can see this, and we will delete all of it once Beta ends.
